commit.message:
Drop unused `sub[IL]_reg_min()` instruct blocks

The `subI_reg_min()` and `subL_reg_min()` blocks from the previous patch
will never be used by C2 since `sub reg, MIN` nodes are rewritten as
`add reg, MIN` in `Sub[IL]Node::Ideal()` during GVN.  Assuming every
`SubI` and `SubL` node goes through GVN, including those created in
subsequent compilation phases, we anticipate that nodes like `sub reg,
MIN` will never occur in the backend.

8317521: Enhance detection of integral unsigned comparisons

This patch makes two changes to the C2 optimizer.  First, it rewrites
`x ^ [INT|LONG]_MIN` to `x + [INT|LONG]_MIN`, so that C2 can better
recognize unsigned comparisons.  Second, during codegen on AArch64, this
patch makes C2 emit `x +/- [INT|LONG]_MIN` expressions using the `EOR`
instruction as opposed to the `ADD` instruction, since the former can
support 32- and 64-bit constants whereas the latter can support only
12-bit constants (thus requiring an additional `MOV` instruction to load
the `INT_MIN` or `LONG_MIN` constants).

More precisely, this patch introduces two new Ideal rewrite rules (in
`XorINode::ideal()` and `XorLNode::ideal()`) and it introduces four new
codegen rules in aarch64.ad.  In the process of adding both ideal graph
tests and post-matching IR tests, this patch also augments the IR node
regexes so that tests can look for newly introduced AArch64-specific
`addI_reg_min` and `addL_reg_min` nodes.

This patch does not make any changes to the X64 backend.  Specifically,
since both `ADDL` and `XORL` instructions accept full 32-bit immediates
and since both `ADDQ` and `XORQ` instructions require a register to load
64-bit constants, there seems to be no benefit to rewriting
`x + [INT|LONG]_MIN` to use `XOR` instructions.
